content_markdown: A system absorbs $70\text{ J}$ of thermal energy and in the process does $40\text{ J}$ of work. The internal energy change is
vibe_explanation: This question tests the First Law of Thermodynamics: $\Delta U = Q - W$. Here, the system absorbs heat ($Q = +70\text{ J}$) and performs work on the surroundings ($W = +40\text{ J}$). Therefore, the change in internal energy is $\Delta U = 70\text{ J} - 40\text{ J} = 30\text{ J}$.
